spending.name: CMS Medicaid Provider Spending Data
spending.note: Fee-for-service (FFS) claims only. Does NOT include managed care capitation payments.

enrollment.name: CMS Medicaid Enrollment - New Adult Group
enrollment.note: Monthly unduplicated enrollee counts (FFS + managed care combined), averaged annually.

managed_care.name: CMS Medicaid Managed Care Enrollment Reports + KFF Survey
managed_care.note: Percentage of Medicaid enrollees in any managed care arrangement (MCO, PCCM, limited-benefit).

expansion.name: KFF Status of State Medicaid Expansion Decisions
expansion.note: 41 states + DC have adopted ACA Medicaid expansion as of 2025.

medicare.name: CMS Medicare Physician & Other Practitioners PUF (2023)
medicare.note: NPI-level Medicare payment data for cross-program comparison.

methodology_notes: FFS CAVEAT: Our spending dataset captures ONLY fee-for-service claims. States with high managed care penetration (e.g., TN 94%, TX 96%) have most Medicaid spending flowing through MCOs, which is NOT in our data. Always consider managed_care_pct when comparing state spending.
Per-enrollee spending is calculated as total FFS spending / total enrollment (FFS + managed care). For states with high managed care, this understates actual per-enrollee spending.
Medicare crosswalk: Providers appearing in both Medicaid and Medicare can be compared across programs. Dual-eligible counts indicate patients enrolled in both programs.
Enrollment counts are 12-month averages of monthly unduplicated enrollee counts from CMS. The 2020-2023 peak reflects COVID-era continuous enrollment requirements.
